mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding economic activity and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment-to-population ratio at cyclical lows in early 2006 supports reflation narrative
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months when PAYEMS expands sequentially, or rank IC < 0.05 over a 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below the 1500 level signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market cooling observed in early 2006 permits threshold calibration
failure_condition: If S&P 500 posts positive returns in more than 50% of months when HOUST < 1500, or the signal's hit rate is below 55% over any 2-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed funds rate is extremely low (near zero) and inflation remains subdued, real discount rates are compressed, boosting equity valuations and producing positive forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical low rate environments post-2001 recession support mechanism
regime_hint: accommodative monetary stance with low inflation
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs but 20-day forward returns are negative in >40% of observations, or the average return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE > 7%), further softening of industrial production indicates deepening economic weakness, depressing corporate profits and stock returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 8.4% qualifies as high unemployment regime
failure_condition: If when UNRATE > 7% and INDPRO declines, S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 40% of cases, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is higher than in normal regimes, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and aggregate supply, which is a leading indicator of economic strength and positive equity returns over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index near 101.4 in February 2006, above prior year
failure_condition: If weekly returns following INDPRO expansion are not positive on average, or the signal's win rate is below 52% over any 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real retail sales suggests weakening consumer spending, which is a primary driver of GDP and corporate earnings, foreshadowing negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at $535,982 million in January 2006 provides baseline for change calculation
failure_condition: If the condition is met but S&P 500 positive returns occur in more than half of the observations, or the average forward return is not negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP grows above trend and the Fed maintains extremely low rates, the combination generates a powerful economic expansion signal that strongly boosts equity prices over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2005 GDP was $17,282 billion, representing ~3.2% YoY growth
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but forward 60-day returns are negative in >30% of cases, or the average excess return over risk-free is not >2%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (core CPI below 2% YoY), a low federal funds rate implies very low real rates, which historically precede equity market rallies as investors search for yield.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI index at 259.7 in Feb 2006 implies year-over-year change of about 1.9% if prior year was 254.9
failure_condition: If under low inflation the coexistence of low FEDFUNDS produces negative average forward returns or a win rate below 55% over any 3-year window, reject.
